metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A newborn can detect and discriminate every language spoken on Earth. Music and language share the same underlying neural infrastructure. Training one of them reinforces the other in the developing brain. Zhao and Kuhl's 2016 study, not primarily about language, revealed this musical reinforcement.

props.artifactTitle: The infant language window and music
artifactLines: A newborn can detect and discriminate every language spoken on Earth.
Music and language share the same underlying neural infrastructure.
Training one of them reinforces the other in the developing brain.
Zhao and Kuhl's 2016 study, not primarily about language, revealed this musical reinforcement.

beats.narration_text: The finding that a newborn can discriminate every language on Earth, and that Zhao and Kuhl's 2016 study wasn't primarily about language yet showed music training reinforcing language processing through shared neural infrastructure, makes me want to understand the mechanism. Help me: (1) explain what 'training one reinforces the other' means for shared circuits; (2) describe what the 2016 experiment actually measured in the infants; (3) clarify why the language window narrows from all sounds to only the native set.

props.segment: Born Bilingual: The Language Window, Neuroscience, a
props.command: The finding that a newborn can discriminate every language on Earth, and that Zhao and Kuhl's 2016 study wasn't primarily about language yet showed music training reinforcing language processing through shared neural infrastructure, makes me want to understand the mechanism. Help me: (1) explain what 'training one reinforces the other' means for shared circuits; (2) describe what the 2016 experiment actually measured in the infants; (3) clarify why the language window narrows from all sounds to only the native set.
output: She can hear every language on Earth
She arrives capable of detecting and discriminating them all
